methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Exodys Energy is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company that has raised $10M+. The valuation universe for nuclear comprises 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method. Using total raised of $10M as a proxy for implied post-money valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-Series A progression yields $25M. This method directly reflects the capital intensity and risk profile of early-stage nuclear technology development.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient comparable data at the Seed stage and similar valuation tier within nuclear energy; while 8 private nuclear peers exist sector-wide, none meet the clustering threshold required for robust median comparison. The single applicable method supports a triangulated range of $21M to $29M, with a median of $25M. Key uncertainties include the company's technology differentiation within the crowded advanced nuclear landscape, regulatory pathway timelines, and capital requirements for commercialization, none of which are resolved by the current data.
